Tonight's guest is Mark Hughes.
Mark, welcome to the show.
Hello, Vic. How's it going?
Well, it's going. How about yourself?

That's good. Beats alternative.

My name's Mark Hughes. I'm 53 years old. I currently live in Australia.
I'm actually just traveling around Australia, just me and my new puppy,
which you'll probably hear in the background at some point.
I'm originally from England. I'm from a place called One House, which is near Stow Market in Suffolk.
I grew up there, a little village. I grew up there. It's all surrounded basically by cornfields and forests.
Not really any livestock or anything, so to speak, really.
Went to the schools in Stow Market. Grew up there until I was roughly around 19.
I got a job, or my dad got me a job on the overhead power lines, which I'd done for roughly about 29 years.
I'd been doing it up to the age of about 39 when I got offered a job with a company called Western Power in Perth, Western Australia, which I then moved over to.
And I've been here for 13 years now and decided to, what with the cost of living and everything,
I just decided, well, I got a really good price for my house, and I've always wanted to travel around Australia.
So I thought, sell up, got the dog and everything.
And now I'm currently in Queensland, about six, seven hours north of Brisbane,
overlooking a nice, lovely lake with mountains in the distance and enjoying life, really, to be honest with you, Rick.
Well, if you can say that, that's all that matters.
That's a really good thing.
Mark, you've got a little helper there who's probably going to be a little bit on
the noisy side as we try to record tonight's show.
Please tell us about him.
Yeah, when I was traveling, it was about four months ago, I have a rescue Rottweiler.
She is my best friend called Precious.
I called her Pudgy because all she had thought about was food and she's my best mate.
And unfortunately, she was seven and a half and she had a problem with her liver.
And unfortunately, you know, it broke my heart.
I had to have her put down while I was traveling.
I was up at a place called Cowley Beach,
up about an hour and a half's drive south of Cairns.
And I must admit, I vowed never to get another dog.
But everyone I spoke to, because I gave Pudgy the best life possible,
even when I was working.
I was walking her three times a day.
She got all the best food, you know,
all the love and care that she ever wanted.
And I never thought I'd get another dog again.
But everyone I spoke to just said, look, you need to get one.
because, you know, you've got to give a dog a good home and everything.
And sort of long story short, I thought, yeah, okay.
So I actually drove down to Brisbane yesterday and picked up a –
I couldn't get another female Rottweiler because I'd be comparing her to Pudgy,
so I brought a male Rottweiler.
He's about six weeks old now.
He's a great little fellow.
I called him Ronnie, Ronnie the Rottweiler.
But he's got loads of food, loads of water.
He's had loads of exercise, loads of tickles now.
but for some reason he's still whinging a little bit.
So he might be getting a little bit vocal every now and again.
So I do apologise for that.
But he a great little fella But he into everything basically And when he gets a bit bored he starts to whinge a little bit That all right Yeah You got your hands

thought, Vic. Um, but they said, I got him from a qualified breeder, um, who obviously breeds

Basically just does what he wants.

shouty sort of dogs.

He's not upset.
